questions.q: In which year was the film Alam Ara, India's first sound film, released?
questions.explanation: Alam Ara was released on 14 March 1931, becoming India's first full-length sound feature film.

questions.q: What distinction does the 1931 film Alam Ara hold in Indian cinema?
questions.explanation: Alam Ara was India's first talkie, the first Indian feature film to include synchronized sound and dialogue.

questions.q: Who directed the 1931 film Alam Ara?
questions.explanation: Alam Ara was directed and produced by Ardeshir Irani, a pioneer of Indian sound cinema.

questions.q: Which production company released the 1931 film Alam Ara?
questions.explanation: Alam Ara was produced and released by the Imperial Film Company, owned by Ardeshir Irani.

questions.q: In which primary language was the 1931 film Alam Ara made?
questions.explanation: Alam Ara was made in Hindi-Urdu (Hindustani), the language of its dialogue and songs.

questions.q: Which song from the 1931 film Alam Ara is regarded as Indian cinema's first film song?
options: De De Khuda Ke Naam Pe
questions.explanation: 'De De Khuda Ke Naam Pe' from Alam Ara is regarded as the first song in Indian cinema history.

questions.q: What is notable about the surviving prints of the 1931 film Alam Ara today?
options: A full restored colour print exists
No print of the film is known to survive
It is preserved in its entirety in London
questions.explanation: No print of Alam Ara is known to survive, making it one of cinema's significant lost films.

questions.q: The 1931 film Alam Ara was based on a popular stage play; what type of source was it adapted from?
questions.explanation: Alam Ara was adapted from a Parsi theatre play written by Joseph David.

questions.q: In which city was the 1931 film Alam Ara primarily produced?
questions.explanation: Alam Ara was produced in Bombay (now Mumbai) at the Imperial Film Company's studio.

questions.q: What does the title 'Alam Ara' translate to in English?
options: Ornament of the World / Light of the World
questions.explanation: 'Alam Ara' translates roughly to 'Ornament of the World' or 'Light of the World', the name of the film's princess heroine.

questions.q: Who played the male lead in the 1931 film Alam Ara?
questions.explanation: Master Vithal, a popular silent-era action star, played the male lead in Alam Ara.

questions.q: Which actress played the title role of the princess in the 1931 film Alam Ara?
questions.explanation: Zubeida played the title role of Princess Alam Ara in the film.

questions.q: Actor Prithviraj Kapoor appeared in the 1931 film Alam Ara in what capacity?
questions.explanation: Prithviraj Kapoor, patriarch of the Kapoor film dynasty, played a supporting role in Alam Ara early in his career.

questions.q: Who composed the music for the 1931 film Alam Ara?
questions.explanation: The music for Alam Ara was composed by Ferozshah Mistri and B. Irani.

questions.q: Which singer-actor is credited with performing 'De De Khuda Ke Naam Pe', the first song in the 1931 film Alam Ara?
questions.explanation: Actor Wazir Mohammed Khan, who played a fakir, sang 'De De Khuda Ke Naam Pe' in Alam Ara.

questions.q: What sound-recording technology was used for the 1931 film Alam Ara?
questions.explanation: Alam Ara used a single-system sound-on-film process, recording sound directly onto the film alongside the image.

questions.q: Because there was no playback technology, how were the songs in the 1931 film Alam Ara recorded?
options: Dubbed in a studio months later
Recorded live during filming with hidden musicians
questions.explanation: Songs in Alam Ara were recorded live during shooting, with musicians playing off-camera since playback singing did not yet exist.

questions.q: On which famous Bombay street did Alam Ara have its premiere in 1931?
questions.explanation: Alam Ara premiered at the Majestic Cinema in Bombay on 14 March 1931 to enormous crowds.

questions.q: The plot of the 1931 film Alam Ara centres on the rivalry between which two figures?
options: A king's two queens (Navbahaar and Dilbahaar)
questions.explanation: The story revolves around the rivalry between a king's two queens, Navbahaar and Dilbahaar, and a prophecy involving a gypsy girl.

questions.q: Roughly how many songs did the 1931 film Alam Ara feature?
questions.explanation: Alam Ara featured about seven songs, modest compared to later films but pioneering for its time.

questions.q: Which genre best describes the 1931 film Alam Ara?
options: A costume fantasy romance with music
questions.explanation: Alam Ara was a costume fantasy romance set in a fictional kingdom, blending royal intrigue, romance and song.

questions.q: Director Ardeshir Irani reportedly raced to complete the 1931 film Alam Ara before which competing sound film could be released?
questions.explanation: Irani rushed Alam Ara to release before rival sound films such as Shirin Farhad, securing its place as India's first talkie.

questions.q: Which imported sound system did Ardeshir Irani use to record the 1931 film Alam Ara?
questions.explanation: Irani used the Tanar single-system sound-recording equipment for Alam Ara, learning its operation largely himself.

questions.q: To avoid extraneous noise, the 1931 film Alam Ara was often shot at what time of day?
questions.explanation: Because microphones picked up surrounding noise from trains and traffic, much of Alam Ara was filmed late at night.

questions.q: Who wrote the original Parsi-theatre play on which the 1931 film Alam Ara was based?
questions.explanation: The play that inspired Alam Ara was written by Joseph David, who was associated with the Parsi theatre.

questions.q: Ardeshir Irani, director of the 1931 Alam Ara, also produced India's first colour film a few years later. Which film was it?
questions.explanation: Ardeshir Irani went on to produce Kisan Kanya (1937), regarded as India's first indigenously made colour film.

questions.q: On what date in 1931 did the film Alam Ara have its premiere?
questions.explanation: Alam Ara premiered on 14 March 1931, a date marked as the birth of Indian sound cinema.

questions.q: The crowds at the 1931 premiere of Alam Ara were so large that what intervention reportedly became necessary?
options: Police had to be called to control the crowds
The film was moved to an open-air ground
questions.explanation: Demand for Alam Ara was so overwhelming that police were reportedly needed to manage the crowds outside the theatre.

questions.q: A sound remake of Alam Ara was attempted in which later year?
questions.explanation: A remake of Alam Ara was produced in 1956, though it failed to match the historic impact of the 1931 original.

questions.q: The arrival of sound with the 1931 film Alam Ara had what major effect on Indian cinema's industry structure?
options: It encouraged regional-language cinemas to develop their own talkies
It made cinema illegal in some provinces
It eliminated the need for actors
questions.explanation: Sound tied films to spoken language, spurring the growth of distinct regional-language film industries across India after Alam Ara.
